title: UK Legislation personas, stories and Explorer interaction journeys
description: Traceability from the six legislation personas and critical journeys to the 100 legal-answer questions, plus browser checks for catalogue discovery before progressive legal-text research.

personas.name: Barrister
personas.need: Locate an operative provision and build a submission with pinpoint official authority.

personas.name: Pupil barrister or paralegal
personas.need: Distinguish principal, secondary, amendment, commencement and draft material.

personas.name: Policy researcher
personas.need: Explore statute-book themes without presenting derived topics as official legal classification.

personas.name: Legislative data engineer
personas.need: Preserve work, manifestation, CLML subdivision and source-native identity.

personas.name: Knowledge curator
personas.need: Refresh the pack while keeping counts, docs, screenshots and validation synchronized.

personas.name: AI operator or evaluator
personas.need: Produce a useful answer with complete proposition-level provenance and temporal context.

stories.user_story: Find the operative provision, state its rule and retain an official selected-passage route.
Apply the correct principal or secondary provision to a disputed fact pattern without inventing facts.
Explore a legal theme through pack-derived topics, then verify candidate relevance in official text.
Resolve structured text while checking version, commencement, extent and amendment state.
Produce a concise answer whose every material proposition has complete official provenance.
Regenerate, validate and publish the pack with documentation and screenshots in lockstep.
stories.coverage_gap: The legal-answer suite evaluates research answers, not curator refresh operations; generator and documentation-lockstep tests cover this story separately.

journeys.title: Find and inspect Human Rights Act 1998 before opening progressive legal text
Reduce legislation by year, sort it and restore the public URL state
